items.original_body: Google today launched a beta test version of Google SMS, this new service will allow US wireless users to query Google for almost any type of information.

items.original_title: Code to exploit Windows graphics flaw now public
items.original_body: Program showing how to use flaw in Windows' handling of graphics files hits the Net--back up your family photos and beware.
items.mutated_title: Code to exploit Windows graphics flaw now public

items.original_title: Appleby leads by a stroke in South Africa
items.original_body: Stuart Appleby of Australia was the only player to break 70 Friday, shooting a 3-under-par 69 that left him with a one-stroke lead over Lee Westwood of England halfway through the Nedbank Golf Challenge.

items.original_title: Nice Day for a Wireless Wedding
items.original_body: Even though we knew it was coming, journalists seem to have plenty to write about now that underdogs Sprint and Nextel have announced their \$35 billion nuptial plans, including a rumor that one of the industry's big dogs would try to spoil the party. -washingtonpost.com

items.original_title: Mubarak seeks comprehensive reforms in Egypt
items.original_body: Egyptian President Husni Mubarak wants reform on the economic, political and social fronts, according to an interview published on the eve of his ruling National Democratic Party #39;s (NDP) annual convention.
items.mutated_title: Mubarak seeks comprehensive reforms in Egypt
items.mutated_body: Egyptian President Husni Mubarak wants reform on the economic, political and social fronts, according to an interview published on the eve of his ruling National Democratic Party #39;s (NDP) annual convention, long after decades of stagnation.

items.original_title: EA to boost efforts in cell-phone gaming
items.original_body: Gaming giant Electronic Arts will boost production of video games for mobile phones over the next nine months, bringing top-selling franchises quot;Fifa Football quot; and quot;The Sims quot; to handsets, the company said Thursday.
items.mutated_title: EA to boost efforts in cell-phone gaming
items.mutated_body: Gaming giant Electronic Arts will boost production for mobile phone video games over the next nine months, bringing top-selling franchises “Fifa Football” and “The Sims” to handsets, the company said Thursday.

items.original_title: Atkins slims down in face of competition
items.original_body: Atkins Nutritionals, the food company founded by the man behind the Atkins diet, is laying off workers and cutting costs in the face of mushrooming competition to supply low-carbohydrate foods.
items.mutated_title: Atkins slims down in face competition

items.original_title: Stonecipher pushes to end Airbus aid
items.original_body: The chief executive of Boeing Co. says his efforts to end government aid programs for rival aircraft manufacturer Airbus SAS are intended to head off a wider trans-Atlantic dispute over subsidies that could end up at the World Trade Organization.
items.mutated_title: Stonecipher pushes to end Airbus aid
items.mutated_body: The chief executive of Boeing Co. says his efforts to end government aid programs for rival aircraft manufacturer Airbus SAS are intended to head off a wider trans-Atlantic dispute over subsidies that could end up at the World Trade Organization, a report notes.

items.original_body: Google Inc. on Thursday launched a beta test version of Google SMS, a new service that lets users of wireless devices query Google for specific information, such as business or residential phone listings, product prices and word definitions, the company announced.
items.mutated_body: Google Inc. on Thursday launched a beta test version of Google SMS, a new service that lets users of wireless devices query Google for specific information, such as residential phone listings, product prices and word definitions, the company announced.

items.original_title: Shares rise after the company reports strong earnings
items.original_body: Investors reacted positively to the quarterly profit report.
